title: 08 — Risk Management and Dignity of Risk
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in hot pink to magenta matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 8
d. Risk Management and Dignity of Risk
dWe will help you try new things safely. We respect your right to take reasonable risks to live the life you want.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dLiving a full life means taking some risks
d[IMAGE: Person on a swing reaching forward ? full of joy, supported]
dTrying new things has some risk.
dThat's a normal part of life.
dYou have the RIGHT to take reasonable risks.
dThis is called 'Dignity of Risk'.
dWhat is dignity of risk?
d[IMAGE: Person climbing stairs with a support worker walking nearby ? supported, not held back]
dDignity of risk means:
d-  You can try new things
d-  You can make mistakes
d-  You can learn from them
d-  DYAR will NOT wrap you in cotton wool
d-  We support you, not stop you
dHow DYAR keeps risks safe
d[IMAGE: Checklist clipboard + safety helmet + supportive worker ? preparing together]
dBefore you try something new we will:
d-  Talk about what you want to do
d-  Look at what could go wrong
d-  Plan how to be safer
d-  Make sure you have what you need
d-  Be there to help if you need us
dExamples of dignity of risk
d[IMAGE: Four people doing things: catching bus, swimming, working, cooking ? full lives]
dDYAR supports you to:
d-  Try catching public transport
d-  Cook your own meal
d-  Go to a concert or sports game
d-  Try a new sport
d-  Live more independently
d-  Date or have relationships
d-  Work or volunteer
dWhen risks are TOO big
d[IMAGE: Yellow caution triangle next to a friendly conversation ? talking about big risks]
dSome risks are too dangerous.
dIf we are worried, we will:
d-  Talk with you honestly
d-  Explain why we are worried
d-  Suggest a safer way to do it
d-  Include your family or advocate
dBut the FINAL decision is still yours ? unless the law says otherwise.
dYour safety net
d[IMAGE: Safety net with a person on it ? they took a leap, they're okay]
dIf something goes wrong we will:
d-  Help you immediately
d-  Not say 'I told you so'
d-  Talk about what to do next time
d-  Try again, smarter
dA life without ANY risk is not a full life. DYAR helps you live a FULL life.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 02 — Individual Values and Beliefs
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in vibrant violet to deep magenta matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 2
d. Individual Values and Beliefs
dWe respect your culture, religion, and beliefs. We will make sure our services reflect what is important to you.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dYour culture matters
d[IMAGE: Circle of people from different cultures ? Aboriginal, Asian, African, European, LGBTQIA+, holding hands]
dEvery person has a culture.
dEvery person has beliefs.
dEvery person has things that matter to them.
dDYAR respects all of these.
dWhat we ask you about
d[IMAGE: Speech bubble with cultural symbols ? prayer hands, food, music notes, family]
dWhen you join DYAR we ask:
d-  What is your background?
d-  What language do you speak?
d-  What is your religion or faith?
d-  What food do you eat? (Halal, Kosher, Vegetarian, etc.)
d-  What are your important days?
d-  What is your gender identity?
d-  What pronouns do you use?
dYou only share what you want to share.
dHow DYAR honours your culture
d[IMAGE: Support worker respectfully listening to participant of different cultural background, with caring smile]
dWe will:
d-  Match you with workers who understand your culture (where possible)
d-  Use interpreters in your language ? for free
d-  Respect your prayer times, fasting, and Sabbath
d-  Make sure food meets your dietary needs
d-  Celebrate days that matter to you
d-  Never make you do anything against your beliefs
dAboriginal + Torres Strait Islander participants
d[IMAGE: Aboriginal flag colours (black, red, yellow) in a circle, with the Whadjuk Country acknowledgement]
dDYAR works on Whadjuk Country.
dWe acknowledge the Traditional Custodians.
dWe follow our DYAR Practice Guide ? Working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Participants.
dLGBTIQA+ participants
d[IMAGE: Rainbow flag with diverse people standing together proudly]
dDYAR welcomes you.
dYour gender + sexuality + relationships are safe with us.
dWe use the pronouns and the name YOU choose.
dIf something at DYAR does NOT respect your culture or beliefs ? please tell us. We will fix it.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 04 — Independence and Informed Choice
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in warm orange to coral matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 4
d. Independence and Informed Choice
dWe will help you make your own choices about your care. We will support you to be as independent as possible.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dIndependence means doing things YOUR way
d[IMAGE: Person walking forward confidently, support worker stepping back ? letting them lead]
dDYAR is here to support ? not to take over.
dWe help you do things by yourself.
dWe do for you only what you cannot do alone.
dInformed choice
d[IMAGE: Brain with multiple pathway icons ? thinking through options]
dInformed choice means:
d-  You know what your options are
d-  You know what each option will look like
d-  You know the good things AND the risks
d-  You decide ? not us
dHow DYAR helps you choose
d[IMAGE: Support worker showing 3 options on a clipboard, participant pointing at one]
dWe will:
d-  Explain things in simple words
d-  Show you pictures or Easy Read papers
d-  Give you time to think
d-  Let you talk to family, an advocate, or your doctor
d-  Repeat anything you do not understand
d-  Use an interpreter ? in any language
dBuilding your skills
d[IMAGE: Step-by-step path of footprints climbing up ? growing skills]
dDYAR helps you learn:
d-  How to make a cup of tea
d-  How to catch the bus
d-  How to manage your money
d-  How to live in your own home
d-  How to make friends
d-  How to look after your health
d-  Anything else you want to learn
dYour right to say NO
d[IMAGE: Stop-hand gesture with red 'NO' sign ? friendly empowering]
dYou can say NO to anything you don't want.
dEven if your worker thinks it's a good idea.
dYour NO is RESPECTED.
dYou are the boss of your own life. DYAR is here to support ? not to decide for you.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 11 — NDIS Code of Conduct
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in bold red to deep red matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 11
d. NDIS Code of Conduct
dAll our workers must follow the NDIS Code of Conduct. This means acting with respect, providing safe supports, and never exploiting or harming you.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dWhat is the NDIS Code of Conduct?
d[IMAGE: Book with NDIS logo and shield ? rule book that protects you]
dIt's a set of rules.
dEvery worker in the NDIS must follow these rules.
dDYAR workers must follow them too.
dThe 7 NDIS Code rules
d[IMAGE: Seven numbered icons in a row ? each one is a rule]
d. Respect your rights
d[IMAGE: Hand on heart ? respect]
dTreat you as a full person. Listen to you. Respect your choices.
d. Respect your privacy
d[IMAGE: Padlock]
dKeep your information safe. Don't share without permission.
d. Provide quality supports
d[IMAGE: Five-star rating]
dWorkers must do their job well. Be trained. Be safe.
d. Act with integrity, honesty and transparency
d[IMAGE: Magnifying glass over a heart ? honest, transparent]
dTell the truth. Don't trick you. Don't hide things.
d. Promptly raise + respond to concerns
d[IMAGE: Speech bubble with an exclamation ? speaking up quickly]
dIf something is wrong ? say so. Fix it fast.
d. Take action to prevent + respond to violence + abuse
d[IMAGE: Shield with cross ? protecting against harm]
dWorkers MUST stop and report violence + abuse + neglect.
d. Take action to prevent + respond to sexual misconduct
d[IMAGE: Hand stopping unwanted contact ? protecting your body]
dNo sexual contact between worker and participant. Workers report any concerns.
dWhat this means for you
d[IMAGE: Participant with a checklist ? knowing your rights]
dYou can expect every DYAR worker to:
d-  Treat you with respect
d-  Be on time
d-  Do what they say they will do
d-  Tell you the truth
d-  Keep your information private
d-  Speak up if they see something wrong
dIf a worker breaks the Code
d[IMAGE: Worker walking away from a building ? meaning they're removed]
dDYAR will:
d-  Take you seriously
d-  Investigate
d-  Remove the worker if needed
d-  Report to the NDIS Commission
d-  Make sure you are safe
dCode of Conduct is YOUR shield. Tell us if any worker breaks it.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 03 — Privacy and Dignity
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in teal-green to emerald matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 3
d. Privacy and Dignity
dWe will always protect your privacy. We will keep your information safe. We will treat you with dignity and respect.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dWhat is privacy?
d[IMAGE: Padlock on a door with a friendly face ? keeping things safe]
dPrivacy means:
d-  Your information is kept safe
d-  Only the right people can see it
d-  No one talks about you behind your back
d-  Your space and body are respected
dWhere DYAR keeps your information
d[IMAGE: Australia map with secure cloud icons connected to it ? meaning Australian data storage]
dYour information is kept in:
d-  Splose (case management ? Australian data centre)
d-  Centro Assist (compliance ? Australian data centre)
d-  Microsoft 365 (email + documents ? Microsoft Australian data centres)
d-  A locked filing cabinet at our office (any paper records)
dYour information is NOT sent overseas.
dWho can see your information
d[IMAGE: Small group of named people: Service Manager + your workers + your accountant (only for billing) ? circle of trust]
dOnly:
d-  Mehdi (Service Manager)
d-  Your DYAR workers ? only what they need
d-  Your family or advocate IF you say yes
d-  Government bodies ? only when the law says so
dAnyone else? Only if YOU give permission.
dWhat is dignity?
d[IMAGE: Person standing tall and proud with the sun behind them ? feeling respected]
dDignity means being treated like a person who matters.
dDYAR workers will:
d-  Knock before entering your space
d-  Speak to you, not over you
d-  Cover and protect your body during personal care
d-  Listen when you say no
d-  Use the name and pronouns YOU choose
d-  Never make fun of you
d-  Never share your story without permission
dYour rights about your information
d[IMAGE: Person holding up a clipboard with rights listed ? checkboxes]
dYou can:
d-  ASK to see what we know about you
d-  ASK us to fix anything wrong
d-  WITHDRAW your permission any time
d-  COMPLAIN if your privacy is broken
dIf you feel your privacy or dignity was NOT respected ? tell us. We take this seriously.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 10 — Transparent Feedback and Complaints
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in warm yellow to amber matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 10
d. Transparent Feedback and Complaints
dIf you are unhappy, please tell us. We welcome your feedback and complaints so we can fix the problem and do better.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dDYAR wants your feedback
d[IMAGE: Person speaking into a megaphone with a friendly speech bubble ? encouraging feedback]
dTell us when things are GOOD.
dTell us when things are NOT good.
dTell us what could be BETTER.
dEvery voice helps us improve.
dYou will NOT get in trouble
d[IMAGE: Shield with a heart inside ? protection for those who speak up]
dIf you complain:
d-  Your supports will NOT stop
d-  Your workers will NOT be cross
d-  You will NOT be punished
d-  Your privacy will be respected
d ways to give feedback or complain
d[IMAGE: Four icons: phone, envelope (email), pen + paper, person-to-person speech bubble]
d. Phone
d[IMAGE: Phone icon]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306.
d. Email
d[IMAGE: Envelope icon]
dEmail hello@dyar.com.au.
d. Form
d[IMAGE: Form icon with pen]
dFill in our feedback form on dyar.com.au.
d. Tell your worker
d[IMAGE: Two people sitting together, one talking, one listening kindly]
dTell your support worker. They will pass it on.
dYou can be anonymous
d[IMAGE: Mask icon ? meaning private, no name needed]
dYou do NOT need to give your name.
dUse our Anonymous Complaint Form.
dDownload from dyar.com.au/feedback.
dAsk someone to help
d[IMAGE: Three people holding hands ? participant + family + advocate]
dYou can ask:
d-  Family or friends
d-  An advocate (see our Advocacy Flyer)
d-  Your support worker
d-  Anyone you trust
dWhat DYAR does next
d[IMAGE: Numbered steps with footprints ? 1, 2, 3, 4 clear pathway]
d. We say thank you within 2 days.
d. We look into your feedback.
d. We tell you what we will do.
d. We do it.
d. We follow up with you.
dIf you are not happy with our response
d[IMAGE: Path leading to a bigger building ? meaning escalation]
dContact:
d-  NDIS Quality and Safeguards Commission: 1800 035 544
d-  Office of Australian Information Commissioner (privacy): 1300 363 992
d-  An independent advocate (see our Advocacy Flyer)
dWe mean it. Tell us. We promise to listen.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 05 — Freedom from Violence Abuse Neglect and Exploitation
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in rich red to burgundy matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 5
d. Freedom from Violence, Abuse, Neglect and Exploitation
dWe will keep you safe. We have a zero-tolerance policy for any kind of violence, abuse, neglect, or exploitation.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dDYAR's zero-tolerance promise
d[IMAGE: Shield with a heart inside ? protective, caring]
dZERO tolerance means:
d-  No violence ? ever
d-  No abuse ? ever
d-  No neglect ? ever
d-  No exploitation ? ever
dIf it happens ? DYAR takes action straight away.
dWhat is violence?
d[IMAGE: Hand raised in 'stop' position ? protecting against harm]
dViolence is when someone hurts your body.
dHitting. Slapping. Pushing. Squeezing too hard.
dNO worker is allowed to hurt you.
dWhat is abuse?
d[IMAGE: Speech bubble with a red cross ? silencing unkindness]
dAbuse is when someone hurts your feelings or your body.
dExamples:
d-  Yelling at you
d-  Calling you names
d-  Touching you in a way that feels wrong
d-  Threatening you
d-  Stopping you doing things you want to do
d-  Making you scared
dWhat is neglect?
d[IMAGE: Empty plate + cold mug ? meaning ignored, not cared for]
dNeglect is when you don't get the support you need.
dExamples:
d-  Not enough food
d-  Not enough water
d-  Not enough warmth
d-  No clean clothes
d-  No medication on time
d-  Being ignored when you need help
dWhat is exploitation?
d[IMAGE: Wallet with red cross ? meaning protecting from financial harm]
dExploitation is when someone uses you for what THEY want.
dExamples:
d-  Taking your money
d-  Using your NDIS funding wrong
d-  Making you work without pay
d-  Making you do things sexual that you don't want
dIf something happens ? what to do
d[IMAGE: Step-by-step path with arrow leading to safety]
d. GO somewhere safe.
d. TELL someone you trust.
d. CALL Mehdi: 0470 540 306.
d. OR call the police: 000.
d. OR call the NDIS Commission: 1800 035 544.
d. KEEP telling until someone helps.
dIt is NEVER your fault. You will NOT get in trouble for speaking up. You will be believed.
dHow DYAR protects you
d[IMAGE: Workers wearing DYAR badges with check-mark icons ? verified safe]
dEvery DYAR worker:
d-  Has a Worker Screening Check
d-  Has a Working with Children Check (where needed)
d-  Has training on keeping you safe
d-  Signs a Code of Conduct
d-  Will be removed straight away if they hurt anyone
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 12 — Reportable Incidents
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in bright orange to deep orange matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 12
d. Reportable Incidents
dIf something serious or dangerous happens, we must report it to the NDIS Commission to keep you safe and investigate properly.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dWhat is a reportable incident?
d[IMAGE: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, paired with a clipboard and phone]
dA reportable incident is something serious that happens.
dDYAR MUST tell the NDIS Commission within 24 hours.
dWhat counts as serious?
d[IMAGE: Five icons in a row: hospital cross, hands raised in warning, broken chain, lock with cross, speech bubble silenced]
dExamples:
d-  Death of a participant
d-  Serious injury
d-  Abuse or neglect
d-  Sexual misconduct
d-  Using a restrictive practice without permission
dWhy this matters
d[IMAGE: Magnifying glass + heart shield ? investigation that keeps people safe]
dReporting:
d-  Keeps you safe
d-  Helps the NDIS Commission watch over all providers
d-  Stops the same thing happening to someone else
d-  Makes services better
dWhat DYAR will do if something happens
d[IMAGE: Step-by-step pathway with caring footprints ? clear action plan]
d. Make sure you are safe.
d. Get medical help if needed.
d. Tell you (or your family / advocate) within 24 hours.
d. Tell the NDIS Commission within 24 hours.
d. Write down what happened.
d. Investigate (find out what went wrong).
d. Make changes so it doesn't happen again.
d. Tell you what we did.
dYou will be supported
d[IMAGE: Caring person holding the participant's hand ? supportive throughout]
dDYAR will:
d-  Stay with you
d-  Offer counselling if you want
d-  Connect you with an advocate
d-  Listen to what you need
d-  Respect your wishes
d-  Keep your privacy
dYou can also tell the NDIS Commission directly
d[IMAGE: Phone with NDIS Commission logo]
dYou don't have to wait for DYAR.
dWebsite: ndiscommission.gov.au
dEmail: contactcentre@ndiscommission.gov.au
dWhat about restrictive practices?
d[IMAGE: Person and door with a lock ? restrictive practice icon]
dRestrictive practices are special supports that limit what you can do.
dExamples: locked medication cabinet, certain doors locked, behaviour support agreements.
dThey MUST be:
d-  Authorised by an NDIS Behaviour Support Practitioner
d-  Agreed by you (or your nominee)
d-  Written in your Behaviour Support Plan
d-  Reported every month to the NDIS Commission
d-  Always working to be REDUCED over time
dReportable Incidents are about keeping you SAFE. Always.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 09 — Cultural Safety and Inclusion
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in sky blue to deep blue matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 9
d. Cultural Safety and Inclusion
dWe provide a safe and welcoming space for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander people, and people from all cultural backgrounds.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dDYAR welcomes you
d[IMAGE: Wide circle of diverse people from many cultures, smiling ? welcome arms open]
dDYAR is a SAFE space for everyone.
dNo matter your background.
dNo matter your colour, language, or faith.
dAboriginal + Torres Strait Islander people
d[IMAGE: Aboriginal flag colours (black, red, yellow) with Whadjuk Country acknowledgement and an Elder respected by all]
dDYAR works on Whadjuk Country.
dWe acknowledge the Traditional Custodians.
dWe pay our respects to Elders past, present, and emerging.
dWhat this means for you
dWe:
d-  Listen and learn from First Peoples
d-  Connect you with Aboriginal-led advocacy and supports
d-  Respect Sorry Business + cultural obligations
d-  Follow our DYAR Practice Guide ? Working with Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Participants
d-  Have First Peoples Disability Network on speed dial: 1800 88 7766
dPeople from CaLD backgrounds
d[IMAGE: Globe surrounded by people from different countries, all smiling, with speech bubbles in different scripts]
dCaLD means Culturally and Linguistically Diverse.
dDYAR speaks Persian/Farsi ? Mehdi is a NAATI-trained interpreter.
dWe also arrange interpreters in any other language ? for free.
dWe will
d-  Match you with workers who understand your culture (where possible)
d-  Respect dietary requirements (Halal, Kosher, etc.)
d-  Respect prayer times and religious days
d-  Use the name + pronouns you prefer
d-  Never assume ? always ask
d-  Connect you with Ethnic Disability Advocacy Centre (EDAC): (08) 9388 7455
dLGBTIQA+ people
d[IMAGE: Rainbow flag held by people standing together proudly ? safe space]
dDYAR is LGBTIQA+ safe.
dYour gender + sexuality + relationships are respected.
dWe use the words YOU use about yourself.
dPeople with religious or spiritual practices
d[IMAGE: Hands in prayer position next to a calendar with religious days marked]
dDYAR respects:
d-  Daily prayer times
d-  Friday Jumu'ah / Sunday church / Sabbath
d-  Ramadan and other fasting periods
d-  Religious holidays and pilgrimages
d-  Spiritual practices (meditation, ceremonies, etc.)
dIf something is NOT culturally safe
d[IMAGE: Speech bubble next to a hand raised in gentle protest ? speaking up safely]
dTell us straight away.
dWe will:
d-  Listen without judging
d-  Apologise if we got it wrong
d-  Fix the problem
d-  Learn so it doesn't happen again
dCultural safety is your right. Not a bonus. Tell us when we fall short.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 07 — Supported Decision Making
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in fresh green to lime matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 7
d. Supported Decision Making
dWe will help you make decisions that affect your life. If you need support from an advocate or family member, we will include them.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dDecisions are yours
d[IMAGE: Person at a crossroads with multiple paths ? they're choosing, supporters standing beside them]
dBig decisions in your life are YOURS.
dDYAR helps you make them ? not for you.
dWhat is supported decision-making?
d[IMAGE: Two people side-by-side at a table, one is the participant making the decision, the other helping]
dSupported decision-making means:
d-  Someone helps you UNDERSTAND your options
d-  Someone helps you WEIGH the choices
d-  Someone helps you TELL others your decision
d-  YOU still decide ? they just help
dWho can be your support?
d[IMAGE: Diverse group: family member, friend, advocate, support worker ? circle of supporters]
dAnyone you trust:
d-  Family
d-  Friends
d-  Your support worker
d-  An independent advocate
d-  Your doctor
d-  Your support coordinator
dHow DYAR makes it easier
d[IMAGE: Information packet with Easy Read documents next to a clock ? meaning take your time]
dWe will:
d-  Use simple words
d-  Show you pictures
d-  Give you Easy Read papers
d-  Give you TIME ? never rush you
d-  Use an interpreter if needed
d-  Repeat anything you ask
d-  Include your supporter in meetings
dBig decisions DYAR might help with
d[IMAGE: Four icons: house (where to live), heart-pulse (health), bag (work/school), people (relationships)]
dExamples:
d-  Where to live
d-  Big health decisions
d-  Money decisions
d-  Choosing services or workers
d-  Education or work
d-  Anything important to you
dIf you have a nominee or guardian
d[IMAGE: Two people holding a paper that says 'Decision Authority' ? supportive nominee]
dSometimes a nominee or guardian helps with formal decisions.
dDYAR will:
d-  Always include YOU first
d-  Talk to your nominee with your permission
d-  Never make a decision without you knowing
dEven with support, the decision is still YOURS. Always.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 06 — Freedom of Expression
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in bright blue to azure matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 6
d. Freedom of Expression
dWe will listen to you. You have the right to share your thoughts. We will always take your views seriously.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dYour voice matters
d[IMAGE: Person speaking with a big speech bubble ? confident, heard, valued]
dYou have the RIGHT to say what you think.
dYou have the RIGHT to ask questions.
dYou have the RIGHT to disagree.
dYou have the RIGHT to be heard.
dWays you can express yourself
d[IMAGE: Four icons in a circle: speaking, drawing, signing (Auslan), pointing at pictures]
dDYAR welcomes all the ways you communicate:
d-  Talking
d-  Writing
d-  Drawing
d-  Auslan or other sign language
d-  Pointing at pictures or PECS
d-  Using a communication device
d-  Showing us through your face or body
d-  Any way that works for YOU
dHow DYAR listens
d[IMAGE: Big friendly ear with sound waves coming in ? active listening]
dWe will:
d-  Give you time to speak
d-  Look at you when you talk
d-  Repeat back what we heard
d-  Ask you 'did I understand?'
d-  Never finish your sentences for you
d-  Never speak FOR you when you can speak for yourself
dSharing what matters to you
d[IMAGE: Heart with a megaphone ? sharing what's important]
dYou can share with DYAR:
d-  Your goals + dreams
d-  Your worries + fears
d-  Your opinions about your supports
d-  Ideas for how we can do better
d-  What you like ? and what you don't
dIf you can't speak in person
d[IMAGE: Phone + envelope + tablet ? multiple ways to reach DYAR]
dYou can also:
d-  Email hello@dyar.com.au
d-  Use the feedback form on dyar.com.au
d-  Ask a family member or advocate to share for you
dWe take your views SERIOUSLY. We do NOT brush them off.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026

title: 01 — Person-Centred Supports
content: d[HEADER-IMAGE: DYAR Rights & Responsibilities header ? gradient band in deep blue to royal purple matching website card colour]
dCM1 PRINCIPLE 1
d. Person-Centred Supports
dWe will always put you first. You have the choice and control over the services you receive.
d[ICON: Red warning triangle with exclamation mark, friendly style]
dPlease note
dThis information might discuss sensitive topics.
dIf you feel upset or uncomfortable, please tell us.
dWe will help you understand this information in a different way.
dOr we will provide support.
dWhat does Person-Centred mean?
d[IMAGE: Person standing in the middle with a circle of arrows pointing inward ? they are the centre]
dYOU are at the centre of everything DYAR does.
dWe start with what YOU want.
dWe listen to what is important to YOU.
dYour goals lead the way. Not ours.
dChoice and control
d[IMAGE: Two paths splitting, with a person confidently choosing one ? bright and empowering]
dYOU choose:
d-  What support you want
d-  When you want it
d-  Where it happens
d-  Who supports you
d-  How it is delivered
dHow DYAR keeps this promise
d[IMAGE: Support worker and participant sitting side-by-side as equals, looking at a plan together]
dWe will:
d-  Ask you about YOUR goals ? every time
d-  Give you choices, not orders
d-  Change our plan if you change your mind
d-  Match you with workers you like
d-  Use the words and language you prefer
d-  Check in with you often ? is this still right?
dIf a worker tells you what to do ? that is NOT person-centred. Tell us.
dWhat you can do
d[IMAGE: Hand raised in a friendly questioning gesture ? speech bubble overhead]
dIt is OK to say:
d-  I want something different
d-  I do not like this
d-  I changed my mind
d-  I want to try something new
d-  I want a different worker
dYou will NOT get in trouble for speaking up.
dWant to talk?
d[ICON: DYAR logo (stylised D) + phone + email icons]
dCall Mehdi: 0470 540 306
dEmail: hello@dyar.com.au
dWebsite: dyar.com.au
dDYAR Pty Ltd ? An NDIS Service Provider on Whadjuk Country ? Easy Read ? V1 19 May 2026
